revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Heiden (Shinto)]][[es:Heiden (sintoísmo)]][[fi:Heiden (šintolaisuus)]][[fr:Heiden (shinto)]][[id:Heiden (Shinto)]][[it:Heiden (architettura)]][[ja:幣殿]][[pt:Heiden (xintoísmo)]][[ru:Хэйдэн]][[tr:Heiden (Şinto)]]
{{Short description|Offertory hall in shrine}} {{Italic title|reason=}} In {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} architecture, a {{nihongo|'''''heiden'''''|幣殿|offering hall}} is the part within a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}'s compound used to house offerings. It normally consists of a connecting section linking the ''[[honden]]'' (sanctuary, closed to the public) to the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' (oratory).<ref name="ja">[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/h/heiden.htm Heiden], [[:en:JAANUS|JAANUS]], accessed on November 17, 2009</ref> In heiden, offerings (''heihaku''), consisting of strips of paper or silk or white and red clothing, are offered to the ''[[kami]]''.<ref>[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/h/heiden.htm Heiden], [[:en:JAANUS|JAANUS]], accessed on June 11, 2024</ref>
If the shrine is built in the ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]'' style,<ref>Also called [[Gongen]]-zukuri</ref> its stone pavement is lower than the floor of the other two rooms, and it is called {{nihongo|''ishi-no-ma''|石の間|stone room}}, hence the name.<ref>[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/h/heiden.htm Heiden], ''Encyclopedia of Shinto'', accessed on November 17, 2009</ref> It can also be called {{nihongo|''chūden''|中殿}} or in other ways, and its position can sometimes vary. In spite of its name, nowadays it is used mostly for rituals.
The size of the heiden also varies considerably and there are some exceptions regarding its location. For example, the Naikū heiden at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]] is located beyond the four walls that surround the sacred quarter.
==References==
